message:
refactor(ft-data): one-pass cumulative dist + num_samples=0 fast path in WeightedRandomSampler

WeightedRandomSampler::indices computed the cumulative distribution
in two passes: first .iter().map().sum() to get the total, then a
second .iter() loop that re-applied sanitize_weight, accumulated a
running total, and divided by the precomputed total to populate
the cumulative vec. The two passes did identical work on the same
weights, and re-ran sanitize_weight twice per element.

Collapse to one pass:
- Build the cumulative vec while accumulating `total` in a single
  scan, applying sanitize_weight once per element.
- After the scan, if total > 0.0, divide each cumulative entry in
  place by total to normalize. The all-zero-weights branch
  (total <= 0.0) still falls through to uniform sampling and
  doesn't pay the divide.

Add a num_samples == 0 fast path that returns an empty Vec before
any allocation or scan — previously the function still walked the
weights and ran the rng loop with `for _ in 0..0`, which produced
the right answer but did unnecessary work (and obscured the
invariant that "no samples requested" should never touch the rng).

The existing weights.is_empty() short-circuit stays first, since
a degenerate-weights call should never construct an empty
cumulative vec and then try to sample from it.

Add weighted_random_sampler_preserves_mixed_weight_sequence as a
fixed-seed regression on the new code path:
- Weights: [1.0, NaN, -5.0, 3.0, +INF, 2.0]
- num_samples=16, seed=123
- sanitize_weight maps NaN/-INF/+INF/negative to 0.0, so the
  effective distribution is [1, 0, 0, 3, 0, 2] which normalizes
  to [1/6, 0, 0, 3/6, 0, 2/6] cumulative [1/6, 1/6, 1/6, 4/6,
  4/6, 6/6].
- Asserts the exact deterministic 16-sample output, so any
  future refactor that reorders cumulative-build vs uniform-fallback
  or that drops a sanitize_weight call surfaces immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
